comment:
Windows 3.11 Setup — oxide86 debug session

Goal: Windows 3.11 Setup completes but fails to launch Windows itself afterward.

Mechanism: Windows 3.x Standard Mode uses 286 protected mode (not 386 Enhanced Mode).
The setup binary (SETUP.EXE) switches to 286 PM to move extended memory, then exits
PM and continues in real mode. If anything goes wrong in the PM round-trip, Windows
never actually starts.

PM entry/exit cycle (confirmed from execution log):
  1. func_005B_35xx (preamble): builds GDT at 000B:0058/0078, far-calls func_005B_0B61
  2. func_005B_0B61 (pm_entry_setup): sets warm-boot vector in CMOS (reg 0x0F=0x09)
     and BDA (0040:0067/0069 = CS:SP at entry), masks IRQs, calls 00CB:0000
  3. 00CB:0000 (pm_entry_stub): LGDT + LMSW → 286 PM; far-jumps to CS=0x0078
     (GDT entry 15, XMS segment 0x11E0 × 16 = physical 0x011E00)
  4. Windows PM kernel runs at 0078:xxxx (sets up GDT, LDT, copies memory)
  5. 0078:0C5D (pm_exit_sequence): LIDT base=0/limit=0 then int3 → intentional
     triple fault → BIOS warm-boot → CPU resets to real mode, BIOS reads CMOS
     0x0F=0x09 and jumps via BDA 0040:0067/0069 back into Windows real-mode code

Fixes applied:
  - BIOS model byte 0xFF (IBM PC) → 0xFC (IBM AT/286): Windows machine-table lookup
    now succeeds; INT 15h AH=0xC0h returns correct model
  - dispatch_exception_pm IVT fallback removed: #GP with no IDT gate now escalates
    to #DF → triple fault instead of looping through the real-mode IVT
  - Triple fault warm-boot: reads CMOS 0x0F; if 0x09, clears PE bit and jumps
    to BDA warm-boot vector (0x0DB5:0x00DE) in real mode instead of halting
  - FF/3 and FF/5 mod=11 panics replaced with #UD exception

Current status / remaining unknowns:
  - Windows Setup still does not launch Windows after completing
  - After the warm-boot return to 0x0DB5:0x00DE the recovery code encounters
    at least one invalid instruction (FF /3 mod=11) which now raises #UD;
    unclear if that or subsequent failures prevent the launch
  - SS:SP after warm-boot reset to 0:0 — Windows recovery code may need the
    original stack intact (the pre-PM PUSHA frame is on it)
  - Further tracing of the 0x0DB5:0x00DE recovery path is needed

005B:0B61.label: pm_entry_setup
005B:0B61.comment:
Windows 3.x Standard Mode 286 protected-mode entry preparation.
Sets ES=0x004B; if setup stage < 3, pushes a real-mode IRET recovery frame
(FLAGS, XMS-seg, 0x0C13), saves own CS as warm-boot recovery segment,
writes warm-boot vector to BDA 0040:0067/0069, copies 6-byte GDT template
from 001B:03FA to ES:0x2A37, programs CMOS reg 0x0F=0x09 (shutdown via
warm-boot vector), saves PIC1 IMR, masks all IRQs, then calls far 00CB:0000.

00CB:0000.label: pm_entry_stub
00CB:0000.comment:
Real-mode stub that enters 286 protected mode.
Executes LGDT (GDT built at 000B:0058/0078 by the 005B:35xx preamble) then LMSW
to set the PE bit, switching the CPU to protected mode.
Far-jumps to CS=0x0078 (GDT entry 15, base=0x011E00 = XMS segment 0x11E0 × 16)
where the Windows PM kernel begins executing.
Not traced in execution log — execution resumes at 0078:xxxx in PM.

0255:03DC.label: a20_query_ps2
0255:03DC.comment:
Query current A20 gate state using the PS/2 fast gate method.
Returns AX=1 if A20 is enabled, AX=0 if disabled (or if PS/2 method is unavailable).
Checks [0x0031] first; if zero, skips the query entirely and returns 0.

0255:0418.label: a20_gate_ps2
0255:0418.comment:
Control or query the A20 gate via PS/2 fast gate (port 0x92, bit 1).
AX=0: disable A20 (clear bit 1); AX=1: enable A20 (set bit 1); AX>=2: query state.
After a set, polls port 0x92 up to 65536 times waiting for the bit to confirm.
Returns AX=1 on success or A20-enabled; AX=0 on timeout or A20-disabled.

0BFF:0147.label: vmm_dispatch_interrupt
0BFF:0147.comment:
Windows VMM cooperative interrupt dispatcher.
Acquires a test-and-set spinlock at ES:[BP] (vmm_ctrl_seg:vmm_ctrl_slot_ptr).
If the lock is free: saves current SS:SP into the control block, walks the linked
list of 8-byte slots to find the queued handler address, then simulates an interrupt
with pushf+call-far, restores SS:SP on return, releases the lock, and irets.
If the lock is busy (lbl_0BFF_01AF): yields without dispatching.

0255:0429.label: a20_disable_path
0255:0429.comment: AX=0: clear AH so bit 1 will be left unset (disable A20), then fall into write path

0078:0C5D.label: pm_exit_sequence
0078:0C5D.comment: Intentional 286 PM exit: load null IDT then fire int3 to force triple fault → warm-boot

lineComments.0255:0418: AX<2 = set operation (0=disable, 1=enable); AX>=2 = query current state
lineComments.0255:041B: preload enable bit mask; doubles as the target state value for polling after an enable
lineComments.0255:042F: merge desired state: AH=0x02 to enable, AH=0x00 to disable
lineComments.0255:0431: i/o delay — pipeline flush before reading back port 0x92
lineComments.0255:0437: CX=0 gives 65536 poll iterations on the first LOOPNZ decrement
lineComments.0255:0441: spin until A20 bit matches desired state or 65536 iterations elapsed
lineComments.0BFF:014A: load segment selector for the control block (GDT entry must cover offset 0x04CC+)
lineComments.0BFF:014F: load current control block slot offset within vmm_ctrl_seg
lineComments.0BFF:0156: test-and-set spinlock: atomically swap 1 in, read old value; 0 = lock was free
lineComments.0BFF:015E: advance control block list pointer by one 8-byte slot
lineComments.0BFF:0164: save current SP into control block for restoration after dispatch
lineComments.0BFF:0168: save current SS into control block (mov [bp+0x04], ss — disasm shows immediate)
lineComments.0BFF:016E: follow linked list: load next pending interrupt's control block offset
lineComments.0BFF:0172: sanity check: next slot's back-pointer must match current slot offset
lineComments.0BFF:018B: simulate an interrupt: push flags then far-call the handler
lineComments.0BFF:0192: reload control block pointer after handler return (ES:[SP] holds it)
lineComments.0BFF:019E: release spinlock: write 0 to ES:[BP] (lock byte)
lineComments.0BFF:01AB: discard the stale return address that was on the stack before the call into this function
lineComments.005B:0B66: check setup stage: ES:[0x0134] < 3 → first-time PM entry path
lineComments.005B:0B8B: check PM-already-entered flag: ES:[0x2F5A] == 0 means first entry
lineComments.005B:0B92: push real-mode IRET recovery frame: FLAGS, XMS segment (0x11E0), return IP (0x0C13)
lineComments.005B:0B9A: AX = CS:[0x0000] = own segment value (0x0DB5), used as warm-boot recovery CS
lineComments.005B:0B9E: if bit 1 of ES:[0x2F16] is clear, take PUSHA save-registers path
lineComments.005B:0BAC: 0x60 = PUSHA: save all general registers (AX CX DX BX SP BP SI DI)
lineComments.005B:0BDD: dest: ES:0x2A37 — GDT copy destination in config segment
lineComments.005B:0BE6: CMOS: disable NMI (bit 7) and select Shutdown Status register (0x0F)
lineComments.005B:0BF5: CMOS reg 0x0F = 0x09: shutdown type = jump via warm-boot vector at 0040:0067
lineComments.005B:0BF7: save current PIC1 IRQ mask before masking all hardware interrupts
lineComments.005B:0BFE: mask all hardware IRQs: PIC1 IMR = 0xFF
lineComments.005B:0C00: enter 286 protected mode via 00CB:0000 (MOVEPM / INT 15h AH=87h stub)
lineComments.005B:3591: 0x8CC8 = MOV AX,CS — load own segment value for GDT physical base calculation
lineComments.005B:3599: rep movsb: copy 0x35EC bytes from 005B:0000 → 004B:DI (relocate setup to XMS segment)
lineComments.005B:35A7: AX=0x11E0 × 0x10 → DX:AX=0x0001:0x1E00 — 24-bit physical base of XMS segment
lineComments.005B:35B8: far jump flushes instruction queue and reloads CS (required before GDT pointer writes)
lineComments.0078:0C5D: LIDT [null]: load IDT with base=0x000000, limit=0x0000 — any exception will triple-fault
lineComments.0078:0C62: int3 fires → #GP (IDT gate 3 beyond limit=0) → #DF → triple fault → BIOS warm-boot → BDA 0040:0067/0069
